page_content:
Formerly: SOCY498D.

SOCY455 Social Dimensions of Privacy and Surveillance (3 Credits) Today, it is quite common in many spheres of life that we divulge personal information in exchange for something else. This course examines practices of surveillance in contemporary society in relationship to collective understandings of privacy. By the end of this course, students will be able to analyze privacy norms and practices in the age of mass surveillance, how surveillance produces inequalities in relation to privacy, and how definitions and regulations of privacy and surveillance change over time.

Prerequisite: 6 credits in SOCY courses; or permission of the department. Credit Only Granted for. SOCY391 or SOCY455.
